source_pr.title: log-backup: fixed the unstable test case TestBasic
source_pr.body: Signed-off-by: Yu Juncen ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: close #36957 Problem Summary: Because of the simulator implementation, there is a probability of about 3%(`1 - (255 / 256)^8`) to fail. ### What is changed and how it works? Now, simulator will never return `0` as the checkpoint. ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: log-backup: fixed the unstable test case TestBasic
failure_signature: log-backup: fixed the unstable test case TestBasic
root_cause_explanation: The fake cluster's checkpoint advancement used `rand.Uint64()%256`, which can be 0, so a region checkpoint could stay at 0 and violate the assumption that the server never returns checkpoint value 0. This makes the test probabilistically fail depending on random values/progress. The PR stabilizes the test by ensuring the checkpoint increment is at least 1 (`%256 + 1`) so progress always happens in the simulation.
fix_pattern: log-backup: fixed the unstable test case TestBasic

evidence: evidence: log-backup: fixed the unstable test case TestBasic
